meaning: <日>adj.绳纹文化的(指日本新石器时代文化,因陶器上普遍饰有绳纹,故名)
examples: This Jomon pot is an extremely important pot.
The Jomon way of life seems to have been pretty comfortable.
That the Jomon had the leisure to create their pots before these changes happened suggests they were smarter and more settled than archaeologists had realised.
The latest discovery pushes the dawn of the Jomon era in Japan back 3, 500 years to a period when glaciers were rampant in much of the northern hemisphere.
